ground_truth_notes: symbols/imports/callgraph.json scoped to a curated subset of core src/click/*.py files plus tests/conftest.py (not the whole repo) -- see suite evaluate.py docstrings. architecture.json hand-authored from reading source directly, independent of OrthoAdapter's own output. impact.json entries sampled from real git history commits (051bb0f, 9f9b149) which predate this manifest's pinned commit; file contents may have drifted slightly since, but the co-change relationships are real and verified against `git show --stat`. retrieval.json questions ask where source-code symbols are defined, but OrthoAdapter.retrieve() currently searches only ContextHub's ingested meta-analysis artifacts (architecture/subsystem/debt notes), not raw source code -- so retrieval metrics on this dataset are expected to score near 0 today. This is an honest, real finding about current Ortho retrieval scope, not a broken suite; kept as-is rather than rewriting the questions to match the tool's current limitation.
